title: Context tp_dealloc clears the pending exception under OOM (Fatal _Py_Dealloc invariant) — Python/context.c
description: `Python/context.c` `context_tp_dealloc` runs `PyObject_ClearWeakRefs` + `context_tp_clear` (HAMT/value decrefs) without saving/restoring `tstate->current_exception`; under OOM a Context is freed with a MemoryError in flight, the teardown clears it, and the gh-89373 `_Py_Dealloc` debug invariant fatals.
notes: Fatal message: "_Py_Dealloc: Deallocator of type 'Context' cleared the current exception". Debug-only signature: the _Py_Dealloc invariant is #ifdef Py_DEBUG, so it fatals on ft_debug_asan and jit (both Py_DEBUG=1) with identical message; on release builds (ft_release, upstream, Py_DEBUG=0) the check is compiled out and the same vehicle surfaces an unrelated downstream OOM segfault (importlib/_itertools _get_values) -> matrix n/a for this signature. Both vehicles show the identical fatal; vehicle 1 stack = entry_points->distributions, vehicle 2 = diagnose.run->inspect. Distinct from OOM-0002 (PyContextVar_Set Py_DECREF(NULL) segfault in cv.set). Minimization: PARTIAL -- vehicle-confirmed, no minimal stdlib trigger isolated (needs exact OOM timing with a raw pending exception at the Context's last decref). Fix direction: bracket context_tp_dealloc body with PyErr_GetRaisedException/PyErr_SetRaisedException (no exception save/restore exists anywhere in context.c).
